objective: Execute the approved P0-P5 task queue for resolving Regular-gate translation/rotation pose jumps by replacing post-hoc limiter/smoothing with camera-path optimization, then validate matrix metrics, bounded-delay behavior, Jetson FIFO output, and mesh-route necessity before documentation closeout.

entry_context.user_approval: The user approved the P0-P5 task arrangement and requested all priorities be included in one loop until complete or blocked.
previous_rejections: pose_smooth_r4 rejected because stabilization became too weak
pose_delta_limiter_lim8 rejected because visible pose jumps remained
lockw100 / intentw20 / intentw100 are matrix-level diagnostic probes only, not accepted candidates
entry_context.accepted_consumer: C++ MMAPI/VPI/NVENC EGLImage FIFO consumer remains the accepted device path; this loop initially changes only matrix/path generation.

fresh_prompt_guard: Do not return to lim6/lim7/lim8/lim10, R radius, soft limiter, or blind LP-weight sweeps.
Do not generate Jetson review videos until matrix-level gates justify the candidate.
Manual visual veto remains authoritative over SR_pose, residual, black-border, handoff, and timing metrics.
If local attempts fail, consult docs/knowledge_base routing and public/open-source references before asking the user.
If the route remains blocked after local and external search, stop the loop and report in chat only.

allowed: This loop tests whether camera-path optimization can remove Regular-gate pose jumps while preserving stabilization and FOV boundaries.
Offline full-sequence results are upper-bound quality probes, not real-time claims.
Bounded-delay sliding-window results are quality/latency trade-off candidates only after matrix metrics pass.
MeshFlowPy may be inspected as a possible mesh warp backend if global path optimization fails with local-motion evidence.
forbidden: claiming full Regular 5/5 pass before human review
claiming full real-time EIS before same-input device evidence
copying internal original text, private links, screenshots, tokens, or credentials into public docs

global_success_criteria: Baseline table clearly marks safe103crop98, R4, lim8, lockw100, intentw20, and intentw100 as accepted/conditional/rejected/diagnostic.
A unified matrix metric script reports path D1/D2/D3, seam jumps, FOV/coverage, and transform deltas for every candidate.
Offline full-sequence path optimization is tested on all five Regular clips and either beats lim8/R4 trade-off boundaries or is marked not viable with evidence.
Bounded-delay sliding-window path optimization is tested only if offline evidence justifies it.
Jetson FIFO output is produced only for matrix candidates that pass matrix-level gates.
Mesh route is opened only if global path optimization fails and residual/local-motion diagnostics justify a spatially varying model.
README, one-page summary, evidence map, long-term context, and active contracts are synchronized at closeout.

current_result.hard_gate_summary: Regular02, Regular03, and Regular05 pass black-border and geometry hard gates. Regular01 and Regular04 are visual-review conditional because gray black-border p95 is high while geometry p95 remains below 1%.
current_result.mesh_route_decision: Do not open mesh yet; global bounded QP improved matrix metrics and passed device/geometry gates. Open mesh only if human review rejects bounded QP and residual-grid diagnostics justify local motion/parallax.

target_metrics.regular05_trans_delta_p95: <= 8.0 px, preferably near or below R4 7.243054 without R4-level stabilization loss
target_metrics.max_abs_d2_path: <= 50% of lim8 baseline at known jump frames when possible
target_metrics.black_border_p95: <= 0.01 after accepted device output and crop policy
target_metrics.regular_gate_visual: at least 4/5 Regular clips show no hard pose-jump veto after candidate review
